data.description:
## Description

Write a failing RED integration test for the `ticket clarity-check` subcommand before the implementation exists.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-clarity-check-dispatcher.sh (new — note: test-ticket-clarity-check.sh already exists and tests the script directly; new file specifically tests dispatcher routing)
- .test-index (modify — append to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-clarity-check-dispatcher.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Test invokes `ticket clarity-check <id>` through the dispatcher (not ticket-clarity-check.sh directly)
- [ ] Test uses --stdin mode equivalent: pipe or fixture ticket JSON that ticket-clarity-check.sh accepts via TICKET_CMD mock
- [ ] Test asserts exit 0 when clarity score passes threshold (score >= threshold)
- [ ] Test asserts exit 1 when clarity score fails threshold (score < threshold)
- [ ] Test asserts JSON output structure: {"score": N, "verdict": "pass|fail", "threshold": T}
- [ ] Tests FAIL (RED) before implementation task T2 runs
- [ ] .test-index updated to add test file to both `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries

## Red Marker
tests/scripts/test-ticket-clarity-check-dispatcher.sh [test_clarity_check_routes_through_dispatcher]

## Notes
ticket-clarity-check.sh uses TICKET_CMD to fetch the ticket when given an ID argument. The --stdin mode reads JSON directly from stdin. For dispatcher testing, use a mock TICKET_CMD that returns fixture JSON, then invoke `ticket clarity-check <id>` to exercise the dispatcher routing. Existing test-ticket-clarity-check.sh tests the script directly — do NOT duplicate those tests; this test only verifies dispatcher routing and exit code passthrough.
data.title: [RED] Write failing integration test for ticket clarity-check dispatcher
